ip_outcome_note: suspected_ip field was empty string; ip_verified field left as-is per spec (triviabot does not clean up empty-suspected-ip edge cases).

trivia_bullets.text: Expanding Ooze's flavor text attributes its growth to consuming 'royal garbage,' placing it in an unnamed urban setting with a named noble (Prince Bolbuss). Prince Bolbuss does not appear in any other known MTG card or official lore source as of MH3's release, making this his sole canonical appearance. [Scryfall; WebSearch: no additional Bolbuss lore found]
sources: WebSearch: no Bolbuss lore found beyond this card

trivia_bullets.text: "It eats the royal garbage. Of course it's growing!" — Prince Bolbuss. [Scryfall]

Expanding Ooze holds approximately 46% inclusion in ooze-tribal Commander decks tracked on EDHREC, with 2,504 total decks. Its counter-on-attack ability targeting any modified creature (not just itself) makes it a support piece under ooze commanders like Xavier Sal and Morophon. [EDHREC: expanding-ooze; EDHREC: oozes tribal page]
sources: EDHREC: expanding-ooze card page (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Randy Gallegos has illustrated over 220 MTG cards spanning nearly three decades, with additional credits on the Middle-earth Collectible Card Game. He is among the most prolific artists in Magic's history. [gatherer.wizards.com artist page via WebSearch; artofmtg.com: Randy Gallegos]

Expanding Ooze combines adapt (from Ravnica Allegiance's Simic mechanic, reused in MH3) with the 'modified creature' condition introduced in Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty, allowing it to function in broad {B}{G} modified decks beyond ooze-tribal contexts. [Scryfall oracle text]
